title: Navitas Semiconductor Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Navitas Semiconductor reported Q1 2026 financial results with a revenue beat that drove the stock up 27.66% to $18.25. The company achieved revenue of $8.6 million, exceeding forecasts, while missing on earnings per share, with management attributing positive market sentiment to strong momentum in high-power semiconductor markets. Key metrics: Revenue was $8.6 million (up 18% sequentially, down from $14 million year-over-year), EPS was a loss of $0.15 versus expected loss of $0.05, Gross margin improved by 30 basis points to 39.0%, Operating expenses were $15 million (down 12.8% year-over-year), Cash position was $221 million with zero debt. Management emphasized the company's transformation toward high-power markets, stating that AI has been the primary catalyst driving momentum and leading to broader adoption of high power solutions. The company targets four segments: AI data centers, energy infrastructure, performance computing, and industrial electrification—representing a $3.5 billion addressable market by 2030. AI infrastructure (combining data center and grid applications) experienced accelerated growth, expanding 50% quarter-over-quarter. For Q2 2026, management projects revenue of $10 million (±$500K) and expects continued sequential growth throughout the year. The company targets profitability when revenues reach the high-$30 million range.
